mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates greater economic output and corporate earnings growth, which drives S&P 500 higher over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 12-month rolling correlation between INDPRO percentage change and subsequent 60-day SPX return is consistently negative or below 0.10 over any 5-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals weakening labour market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE rises above 5.0%, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or if the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and consumer price inflation indicate that the central bank is tightening policy to combat rising prices, which compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and lower real earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint signal from rising FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL (both month-over-month increases) fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a Sharpe ratio below -0.20 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In the early-cycle expansion phase after a recession, rising retail sales (RSAFS) reflect strengthening consumer demand that drives corporate earnings and equity prices higher over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: early-cycle expansion (GDPC1 growth accelerating from trough)
failure_condition: If RSAFS monthly growth exceeds 0.5% during early-cycle periods but the median 20-day SPX return is not positive and statistically significant (sign test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll gain below 100,000 jobs signals abrupt labor market weakness that spooks investors and triggers risk-off sentiment, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day SPX return following weak payroll reports is not negative or if the proportion of negative returns does not exceed 55% over any 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and industrial production reflect broad-based economic momentum that boosts corporate profits and equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the composite signal (both HOUST and INDPRO above their 6-month moving averages) does not yield a positive mean 60-day SPX return and a win rate above 60% over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment (CPI YoY above 4%), any increase in the federal funds rate strongly contracts equity valuations as investors price in aggressive tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If during months with CPI YoY above 4%, a one-time 25bp increase in FEDFUNDS does not produce a negative average 20-day SPX return that is significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.5 million units (annualized) indicate very strong real estate demand that often signals late-cycle overheating, leading to negative forward returns as market anticipates policy tightening and eventual slowdown.
failure_condition: If after HOUST exceeds 1.5 million, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is positive in more than 50% of instances, or if the mean return is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).
